note: VERF adds a label-based Views filter for entity reference fields via hook_views_data_alter(), backed by the `verf` filter plugin (extends InOperator). No admin form, routes, permissions, or Drush. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I installed the VERF module. In a View, how do I let visitors filter content by an entity reference field using the referenced entity's title instead of its ID? Where is the setting?
evals.expected_output: Enable verf, then in the View's Add filter criteria list pick the field entry labeled '{Field label} (VERF selector)' and expose it; its value control lists entity labels rather than numeric IDs. There is no separate admin config page.

evals.prompt: What is the admin configuration route/path for the VERF (Views Entity Reference Filter) module?
evals.expected_output: There is no configuration route or admin form (configure is null; no verf.routing.yml, no permissions, no Drush). All configuration happens inside the Views UI on the filter handler.

evals.prompt: Using a VERF exposed filter, how do I limit the selectable options to only certain bundles of the target entity type (e.g. only Article and Page nodes)? Which option key stores this?
must_contain_any: Target entity bundles to filter by
evals.expected_output: In the filter handler settings check the bundles under 'Target entity bundles to filter by' (option key verf_target_bundles); it only appears when the target entity type is bundleable, and limits the option list to the chosen bundles.

evals.prompt: A VERF filter is hiding entities the current user cannot view. Which option makes it list those entities anyway, and what is its machine name?
evals.expected_output: Enable the 'Ignore access control' checkbox (option key show_unpublished, default FALSE). When off, only entities the user can 'view' are listed; when on, all target entities appear.

evals.prompt: In a VERF filter, what happens to an entity when the user may see its label but not view the entity itself?
evals.expected_output: getValueOptions() checks 'view label' access; when the user lacks it the option renders as '- Restricted access -' (the row is kept but the entity's identity is hidden). Labels are also switched to the current content-language translation and the list is natcasesort()-ed.

evals.prompt: Which hook does the VERF module invoke so my custom module can add or remove entities from a VERF filter's option list?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_verf_entites_options_alter(array &$entities) — invoked via moduleHandler->alter('verf_entites_options', ...). Note the misspelling 'entites'. It fires for all VERF filters (no per-view context), passing the loaded referenceable entities.

evals.prompt: What Views filter plugin backs VERF, what core class does it extend, and what is its plugin id?
evals.expected_output: Drupal\verf\Plugin\views\filter\EntityReference, annotated @ViewsFilter("verf") (plugin id 'verf'), extends core Views InOperator. hook_views_data_alter() attaches it as '{column}_verf' filters on entity_reference fields (configurable and SQL base fields).
